purpose: Physics-based digital twin of the motor temperature sensor. Models sensor accuracy and signal quality degradation over time and environmental stress, produces a motor temperature reading used for thermal protection, and raises events for motor overheat and sensor disconnection.

outputs.name: motor_temperature
outputs.description: Reported motor temperature, subject to sensor accuracy and signal quality degradation bias.

states.name: sensor_accuracy_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: long-term drift over thousands of operating hours due to thermal cycling and environmental exposure
states.description: Fractional accuracy health of the temperature sensor (1.0 = fully accurate, 0.0 = fully degraded). Decreases as thermal cycling and mechanical wear shift the sensor calibration over time.

states.name: signal_quality_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: medium-term degradation over hundreds to thousands of operating hours from connector corrosion, cable fatigue, and EMI exposure
states.description: Fractional signal quality health of the sensor output channel (1.0 = clean signal, 0.0 = fully degraded). Decreases due to thermal stress and oxidation causing amplitude and phase deviations in the measurement signal.

states.name: true_motor_temperature
states.description: Internal tracking state representing the estimated true motor temperature. Follows a nominal ambient-to-operating temperature profile via first-order lag. Used as the base signal from which the degraded sensor reading is derived.

events.name: motor_overheat
events.description: Motor overheat alarm triggered when the tracked motor temperature exceeds the critical overheat threshold (140.0 °C). Event state equals 1.0 under healthy conditions and drops toward 0.0 as temperature approaches the threshold.

events.name: temperature_sensor_disconnected
events.description: Temperature sensor disconnection alarm triggered when signal quality degradation falls below the disconnection threshold, indicating loss of a valid measurement signal. Event state equals 1.0 under healthy conditions and drops toward 0.0 as signal quality degrades.

parameters.name: sensor_accuracy_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which sensor accuracy degrades per unit time step, driven by thermal cycling and wear. Derived from preload_degradation_rate as the closest semantic match for thermally-driven calibration drift.

parameters.name: signal_quality_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which signal quality degrades per unit time step, driven by thermal stress and oxidation. Matched to grease_degradation_rate as the closest semantic match for oxidation-driven signal channel deterioration.

parameters.name: temperature_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient controlling how quickly the tracked motor temperature approaches its target value. Capped at 0.5 for numerical stability.

parameters.name: nominal_operating_temperature
parameters.description: Nominal steady-state motor operating temperature used as the target for the first-order lag tracking state.

parameters.name: overheat_threshold
parameters.description: Motor temperature threshold above which the motor_overheat event is triggered. Sourced directly from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_temperature
parameters.description: Absolute critical motor temperature representing full failure boundary. Sourced directly from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: sensor_disconnection_threshold
parameters.description: Signal quality degradation health level below which the sensor is considered disconnected or non-functional.

parameters.name: accuracy_bias_scale
parameters.description: Maximum temperature bias (in °C) introduced into the sensor reading when sensor_accuracy_degradation reaches 0.0 (fully degraded).

parameters.name: signal_noise_scale
parameters.description: Maximum additive noise amplitude (in °C) introduced into the sensor reading when signal_quality_degradation reaches 0.0 (fully degraded).

parameters.name: thermal_cycling_driver_value
parameters.description: Normalized driver intensity for thermal_cycling_and_wear, representing the effective per-step thermal cycling stress applied to sensor_accuracy_degradation.

parameters.name: thermal_stress_oxidation_driver_value
parameters.description: Normalized driver intensity for thermal_stress_and_oxidation, representing the effective per-step oxidative thermal stress applied to signal_quality_degradation.

state_transition_logic: sensor_accuracy_degradation: decrease by sensor_accuracy_degradation_rate * thermal_cycling_driver_value * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
signal_quality_degradation: decrease by signal_quality_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_oxidation_driver_value * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].

true_motor_temperature: approach nominal_operating_temperature using first-order lag with coefficient temperature_lag_coefficient (capped at 0.5): true_motor_temperature += temperature_lag_coefficient * (nominal_operating_temperature - true_motor_temperature) * dt; clamp to [-40.0, 200.0].
output_logic: motor_temperature: computed as true_motor_temperature + accuracy_bias (from sensor accuracy degradation) + signal_noise (from signal quality degradation).

accuracy_bias = accuracy_bias_scale * (1.0 - sensor_accuracy_degradation): as sensor_accuracy_degradation degrades from 1.0 to 0.0, a positive bias of up to accuracy_bias_scale degrees is added to the reported temperature.

signal_noise = signal_noise_scale * (1.0 - signal_quality_degradation): as signal_quality_degradation degrades from 1.0 to 0.0, an additive noise term of up to signal_noise_scale degrees is introduced.
Therefore: motor_temperature = true_motor_temperature + accuracy_bias_scale * (1.0 - sensor_accuracy_degradation) + signal_noise_scale * (1.0 - signal_quality_degradation).
Under healthy initial conditions (both degradation states = 1.0), motor_temperature = true_motor_temperature with zero bias and zero noise.
event_logic: motor_overheat (SP0001): event_state = max(0.0, 1.0 - max(0.0, true_motor_temperature - overheat_threshold) / (critical_temperature - overheat_threshold)). Event_state = 1.0 when true_motor_temperature <= overheat_threshold (140.0 °C); event_state = 0.0 when true_motor_temperature >= critical_temperature (150.0 °C). Threshold is crossed (event fires) when event_state <= 0.0.

temperature_sensor_disconnected (SP0006): event_state = max(0.0, (signal_quality_degradation - sensor_disconnection_threshold) / (1.0 - sensor_disconnection_threshold)). Event_state = 1.0 when signal_quality_degradation = 1.0 (fully healthy); event_state = 0.0 when signal_quality_degradation <= sensor_disconnection_threshold (0.1). Threshold is crossed (event fires) when event_state <= 0.0.
assumptions: The temperature_sensor component has no inputs defined in the triage entry. The true_motor_temperature tracking state is introduced as an internal state to provide a physically meaningful base signal for the output, since no external temperature input is provided.

sensor_accuracy_degradation_rate (0.015) is matched to preload_degradation_rate from the simulator ground truth as the closest semantic analog for thermally-driven calibration drift in a sensor. No direct simulator rate for sensor accuracy was provided.

signal_quality_degradation_rate (0.03) is matched to grease_degradation_rate from the simulator ground truth as the closest semantic analog for oxidation-driven signal channel deterioration. No direct simulator rate for signal quality was provided.
The driver for sensor_accuracy_degradation is thermal_cycling_and_wear, consistent with the simulator ground truth driver list for preload_degradation (the matched rate).
The driver for signal_quality_degradation is thermal_stress_and_oxidation, consistent with the simulator ground truth driver list for grease_degradation (the matched rate).

thermal_cycling_driver_value and thermal_stress_oxidation_driver_value are normalized to 1.0 by default, representing full-intensity driving conditions. These can be tuned if partial-load profiles are available.
The overheat_threshold (140.0 °C) and critical_temperature (150.0 °C) are taken directly from the simulator ground truth.

The sensor_disconnection_threshold (0.1) is not present in the simulator ground truth and is set conservatively to represent near-total signal loss. This value should be validated against observed fault data.

accuracy_bias_scale (10.0 °C) and signal_noise_scale (5.0 °C) are physically motivated estimates for a degraded industrial temperature sensor. These are tunable parameters and should be calibrated against real sensor drift data.
The true_motor_temperature tracking state uses a nominal_operating_temperature of 75.0 °C as a default steady-state target. This should be updated to reflect actual machine operating conditions.
Under healthy initial conditions (sensor_accuracy_degradation = 1.0, signal_quality_degradation = 1.0, true_motor_temperature = 25.0 °C), all event_state values equal 1.0 as required.

The candidate_states in the triage entry listed initial_value as 0.0, which is inconsistent with the degradation state_type convention (1.0 = healthy). Initial values have been corrected to 1.0 for both degradation states per the ProgPy spec rules.
